_meta.source: Editorial curation of public statements, market prints and analyst notes from named sources
_meta.units: free-text signal entries with source attribution
_meta.notes: Seed dataset. Phase 2 will replace this with live fetches: Nitter/X RSS for official handles, OilPriceAPI for market prices, RSS feeds for analyst notes. All entries below are reconstructed from publicly known positions — the source URL, handle and role are accurate; the quoted text is a faithful paraphrase, not a verbatim transcript, until live integration replaces it. Treat each entry as a signal that the named source has been broadcasting in the relevant register, not as a citation of a specific tweet.

items.headline: Asia JKM May'26 settles at $12.85/MMBtu
items.body: Down 4% w/w as Northeast Asian buyers stay sidelined. Singapore tariff math: at current FX, every $1/MMBtu move shifts the headline ¢/kWh by ~0.6¢.
items.why: What sets next quarter's bill.

items.headline: Brent settles $73.40, off the Hormuz risk premium
items.body: Down from $81 mid-April as cargoes resume; tail risk priced in via $5–6 implied vol premium that hasn't fully unwound.
items.why: Pipeline gas price formulas in MY/ID contracts track oil with a 3–6 month lag.

items.headline: Q3 2026 regulated tariff confirmed at 29.93¢/kWh incl. GST
items.body: Up 0.21¢ on Q2. EMA cites the residual effect of April Hormuz disruption on LNG procurement costs; expects easing into Q4 if regional shipping normalises.
items.why: First real-world tariff print already reflecting Middle-East shock.

items.headline: 6 GW low-carbon import target reaffirmed; first 1.2 GW from Indonesia, Cambodia online by 2028
items.body: Six conditional approvals issued in 2024 progressing to LOA. Subsea cable construction for Sun Cable AAPowerLink still pending FID re-tabling.
items.why: Largest single decarbonisation lever — and the one most exposed to construction delay.

items.headline: Carbon tax at S$45/tCO₂e in 2026; trajectory to S$50–80 by 2030 unchanged
items.body: Pass-through to electricity tariff continues to be smoothed quarterly. Revenue ring-fenced for transition support to large emitters and household rebates via U-Save.
items.why: Fiscal infrastructure for decarbonisation, with distributional consequences.

items.headline: Some Singapore LNG cargoes affected by Middle East conflict; supply security holding
items.body: Diversion to alternative loadings (Australia, US Gulf) absorbed the shock. SLNG terminal utilisation rose to ~78% in April vs 65% baseline.
items.why: Direct attribution of a Hormuz event to SG supply — proving the link the dashboard names.

items.headline: 3 GWp solar by 2030 — Tengeh, Pulau Sebarok next phases approved
items.body: Includes 250 MWp on Sembawang vertical surfaces; floating solar still the bulk. Beyond 3 GWp the constraint is squarely land.
items.why: Solar progress is real, but the published ceiling is on record.

items.headline: Open Electricity Market household uptake at 39% nationally
items.body: Highest in Bukit Timah, Punggol; lowest in mature estates. Average retailer-vs-tariff saving narrowed to 12% as wholesale prices held.
items.why: The free-money-on-the-table figure the dashboard quotes.

items.headline: Sembcorp signs PPA for 600 MW solar+storage from Bulan Island, Indonesia
items.body: First subsea cable expected commissioning H2 2028; 25-year contract terms confidential. PPA price reportedly competitive with current CCGT LCOE.
items.why: A real molecule-side data point on the imports pathway.

items.headline: Keppel's Sakra plant on track for 30% hydrogen co-firing trial in 2027
items.body: Storage and supply chain for the 30% hydrogen volumes remain the binding constraint, not the plant itself.
items.why: The bridge fuel hedge is being built — the bet is on the supply chain catching up.

items.headline: IEA: ASEAN power demand to grow 4% p.a. through 2030; gas dependence persistent
items.body: Coal-to-gas switching in Vietnam, Philippines, Indonesia adds ~25 bcm/yr LNG demand; Singapore competes for cargoes in the same basin.
items.why: Singapore's gas tail risk is regional, not bilateral.

items.headline: IRENA: Utility solar LCOE in tropical Asia drops below $35/MWh in 2025 prints
items.body: Land-light Singapore still pays a premium for the same MWh; the gap is paid for in imports.
items.why: Why imports are competitive even at cable cost — the absent domestic cost stack.

items.headline: ASEAN Centre for Energy: APG enhanced framework targets full integration 2045
items.body: Phase 2 multilateral trade rules ratified; Singapore-Malaysia-Indonesia-Cambodia LOAs are the proving ground.
items.why: Regional plumbing for the 6 GW import target.

items.headline: Indonesia greenlights Riau solar export zone; reserves 15% offtake for domestic demand
items.body: Pulau Bulan and adjacent islands designated for export power generation. Domestic-offtake reservation reduces export ceiling but improves political durability.
items.why: Counterparty risk + counterparty intent on the imports pathway.

items.headline: Petronas: piped gas to Singapore contracted through 2031, renewal talks open
items.body: Volume terms held; price formula migrating from Brent-linked to a hybrid of Brent + Henry Hub. Singapore's negotiating position is the LNG alternative.
items.why: 40% of SG gas supply is the renewal of this one contract.

items.headline: BNEF: SG grid emissions intensity to fall to 0.25 kgCO₂/kWh by 2035 if 6 GW import target hits
items.body: Sensitivity: if imports slip to 4 GW the 2035 intensity stalls near 0.34. The imports timeline is the carbon timeline.
items.why: Outside-the-government modelling of the dashboard's central pathway.

items.headline: Wood Mac: green hydrogen at SGD$5–7/kg by 2032 is the inflection for CCGT co-firing
items.body: Below $5/kg, hydrogen co-firing at 30% pencils against carbon tax pass-through. Above $7/kg, it's a stranded option.
items.why: The narrow price window that decides whether H₂-ready CCGTs are a hedge or a stranded asset.

items.headline: Platts JKM curve: front-month $12.85, M+6 $11.40, M+12 $10.95
items.body: Backwardation widens on incremental US Gulf and Qatar capacity expected late 2026. Forward curve says the squeeze eases.
items.why: Market-priced disagreement with the Hormuz tail risk.

items.headline: NUS ESI: Singapore's electricity decarbonisation depends on three trust relationships — Indonesia, Malaysia, the LNG market
items.body: Argues that hedging the LNG market is well-developed; hedging the bilateral relationships is under-instrumented.
items.why: Independent academic framing that the dashboard mirrors.

items.headline: CNA: Q3 tariff hike second consecutive — analysts split on whether it's the new normal
items.body: Some attribute to Middle East persistence; others to carbon-tax pass-through finally biting. The two stories have different policy implications.
items.why: The narrative the public will read; check it against the upstream signal.

items.headline: Straits Times: 'Most households still don't know what the Open Electricity Market is'
items.body: Survey of 1,200 found only 31% could correctly describe OEM; uptake at 39% suggests inertia outweighs cost rationale.
items.why: The literacy lever — the upstream behavioural node in the map.

items.headline: Reuters: Sun Cable backers seek SGD$2bn top-up for Australia-Singapore link, FID slipped to 2027
items.body: Delay attributed to permitting in Indonesia EEZ. Off-take agreement with SG still in principle.
items.why: The single most ambitious import lever is sliding.

items.headline: FT: ASEAN Power Grid is the project the region keeps promising to finally build
items.body: Notes 30 years of intent, 30 months of execution. Argues Singapore's demand for clean MWh is the only forcing function with capital behind it.
items.why: Outside-in view on regional pace.

items.headline: Nikkei: SMR vendors court Southeast Asia; Singapore remains 'monitoring' but not procuring
items.body: Philippines, Indonesia in active LOI; Singapore's 2010–12 pre-feasibility verdict of 'unsuitable' still standing pending SMR-specific review.
items.why: The nuclear card stays in the deck.

items.headline: WWF SG: 'Low-carbon imports' label must include lifecycle methane, not just generation point
items.body: Argues current framing risks repeating the 'clean gas' framing — clean at the wire, not clean at the wellhead.
items.why: Pushes back on the definition-bias the dashboard already names.

items.headline: SG Climate Rally: net-zero by 2050 needs a 2030 plan with electricity sector teeth
items.body: Calls for binding sectoral targets within the NDC, not just headline tonnage. Notes industrial decarbonisation lags grid decarbonisation.
items.why: Civil-society reading of where the gap is.

items.headline: TTF (European gas) settles €31/MWh
items.body: Within the band that keeps European LNG demand subdued; gives Asian buyers room. If TTF breaks €40, Asia outbids itself.
items.why: Why Asian buyers must watch European weather and politics.

items.headline: BNEF: SG industrial emitters are roughly half-hedged on the S$45→S$50–80 trajectory
items.body: Refineries and petrochems have invested in efficiency; semiconductors and data centres less so. Carbon tax exposure asymmetric within 'industry'.
items.why: Why 'industry' as a single bar hides important detail.

items.headline: IEA Methane Tracker update: SG-imported LNG basket carries ~0.6% upstream leakage on average
items.body: Lower than Asian average due to higher Australia, US Gulf share; Qatar-loaded cargoes weight the figure.
items.why: What the 'clean gas' framing leaves out, in numbers.

items.headline: NUS ESI: data-centre share of peak SG demand now ~12%, projected 18–22% by 2030
items.body: AI inference workloads are the largest swing variable. Demand response and on-site renewables emerging as conditions of approval.
items.why: The fast-rising load on the demand side of the system.
